wrong_because: Failed to verify YouTube upload compliance requirements (missing medical disclaimers, AI disclosure, hashtag placement, and 500-char tags list) before presenting script. Also hallucinated personal protocol (CJC+GHK) instead of using verified actual protocol (GHK+BPC-157).
fix: Rewrote script to use factually accurate protocol and appended the exact YouTube metadata block required by upload_quality_gate/SKILL.md.
prevention_rule: Never invent or hallucinate Wayne's medical/health protocols. Always verify against Research_Archives or his explicit past instructions. Always append the full YouTube metadata block (with disclaimers and AI disclosures) to every script output for copy/pasting.

wrong_because: Presented scripts missing Facebook metadata, Instagram metadata, AI Twin disclosure, and 500-char tags. Had to be corrected 4 times in one session. Each correction costs Wayne real money in wasted API credits and generation time.
fix: Created a mandatory 6-section checklist: Script, B-Roll, Thumbnail, YouTube Metadata (title/desc/disclaimer/AI disclosure/hashtags/tags), Facebook Metadata, Instagram Metadata. All 6 sections MUST be present before presenting any script.
prevention_rule: Every short script deliverable MUST include all 6 metadata sections. Run the checklist mentally before finalizing. Cross-reference upload_quality_gate/SKILL.md and the new mandatory_short_production_checklist stored in the content_pipeline brain namespace.

wrong_because: Failed to double-check visual changes via screenshot before confirming to Wayne that work was done. Video sizing, logo sizing, and layout issues were reported by Wayne multiple times.
fix: Established a hard rule: Always take a screenshot and verify visual output before confirming any UI/web change is complete.
prevention_rule: ALWAYS pull a live screenshot via chrome-devtools-mcp take_screenshot after deploying any visual change. Never tell Wayne it is done until the screenshot confirms it matches his instructions.
error: Failed to double-check visual changes via screenshot before confirming to Wayne that work was done. Video sizing, logo sizing, and layout issues were reported by Wayne multiple times.
prevention: ALWAYS pull a live screenshot via chrome-devtools-mcp take_screenshot after deploying any visual change. Never tell Wayne it is done until the screenshot confirms it matches his instructions.

tried: Deploying new website structure without explicitly whitelisting AI bots
wrong_because: Rank Math built-in robots.txt does not whitelist AI bots like GPTBot, meaning AI Generative Engines might drop the site from their context. Missing regional citations also hurt local dominance.
fix: Injected a robots_txt WordPress filter into functions.php to explicitly allow AI bots, and added 7 directory citations into the sameAs array.
prevention_rule: When deploying a new website or performing an SEO audit, ALWAYS ensure an explicit robots_txt AI bot whitelist is applied, and verify that the local schema contains maximum local directory citations.
error: AI bots not whitelisted in robots.txt and missing local citations in schema
prevention: Always explicitly allow AI crawlers (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, etc) in robots.txt via functions.php on any new WP deployment.

tried: Downloading Google Flow videos in batch/silent mode
wrong_because: Upscaled 1080p clips ('_upsampled' suffix) sometimes fail to generate or return a 404 error from storage, causing downloads to fail and leaving clips missing in the target folder.
fix: Implemented a 720p fallback check. If the upsampled download returns a 404 error, strip the '_upsampled' suffix from the API request URL to fetch the raw 720p version, ensuring all generated clips are successfully captured.
prevention_rule: When downloading Google Flow videos, always check for 1080p upsampled files first, but immediately fall back to the raw 720p filename without suffix if the 1080p request returns a 404 to ensure complete downloads.
error: Upscaled 1080p files sometimes return 404 from storage causing missing files
prevention: Always implement a 720p fallback (without '_upsampled' suffix) to ensure all generated media is captured if upscaling fails.

tried: Setting up the voice bridge client dynamically in new sessions
wrong_because: Conversation ID, LS port, and CSRF token changed with every chat initialization, causing the standalone voice bridge server to disconnectedly target stale sessions.
fix: Implemented automatic update of voice_bridge_config.json on workspace bootstrap via scripts/update_voice_config.py using environment parameters.
prevention_rule: Always invoke scripts/update_voice_config.py at the absolute start of the workspace bootstrapping process to sync ports and token credentials.
error: Dynamic session tokens caused the voice bridge to communicate with stale chat frames
prevention: Update the voice bridge configuration using update_voice_config.py during the master bootstrap routine.

tried: Dimming screens all the way down to low using DDC/CI commands
wrong_because: Rapid brightness command transitions (like setting values to 5 or 0) can cause the monitor's built-in OSD scalar firmware to glitch and enable the hardware-level GamePlus crosshair/bullseye.
fix: Advised the user to toggle off the hardware-level crosshair using the monitor's physical joystick OSD control menu, and documented the limitation.
prevention_rule: When executing screen dimming commands, warn users that rapid DDC/CI signaling might glitch-enable internal monitor OSD overlays (like crosshairs) which can only be disabled manually via physical monitor controls.
error: Setting low brightness values via DDC/CI glitches monitor firmware, enabling hardware OSD crosshair
prevention: Avoid values lower than 5% and inform the user that any hardware-level OSD side effects must be turned off using the physical screen buttons.

error: Voice Bridge double glitch on new chats
fix: Modified voice_bridge.py to dynamically get_latest_conversation_id() and removed bridge.deactivate() from session_monitor
fingerprint: voice bridge glitching going out on new chat

error: Playwright CDP connection hangs or fails when Chrome DevTools MCP is holding port 9222 connection
fix: Decoupled the download workflow: execute asynchronous context menu clicks directly inside Chrome via evaluate_script JS tool, and run a local python daemon to fuzzy match, rename, and move downloaded files by prompt text

error: Connecting to Electron DevTools active port WebSocket using 'localhost' causes socket timeouts on Windows
fix: Replaced 'localhost' with '127.0.0.1' in the WebSocket URL before connecting to prevent Windows IPv6 DNS resolution issues

wrong_because: DaVinci Resolve UI was hidden behind the Chrome browser window, causing clicks to hit the wrong screen coordinate
error: [orchestration] DaVinci Resolve UI was hidden behind the Chrome browser window, causing clicks to hit the wrong screen coordinate
fix: ALWAYS bring DaVinci Resolve to the foreground before performing any UI automation or click actions
prevention: ALWAYS bring DaVinci Resolve to the foreground before performing any UI automation or click actions
prevention_rule: ALWAYS bring DaVinci Resolve to the foreground before performing any UI automation or click actions

tried: Downloading Google Flow videos in batch
wrong_because: Renaming cards in Flow UI changes modification times and randomizes DOM indexes. Standard downloads get mixed up. Also, triggering 1080p downloads makes cards jump to the top, while notification toasts intercept mouse hovers/clicks.
fix: Hided notifications via CSS injection. Implemented the rotating spot download algorithm that scans from the bottom card slot (where undownloaded cards rotate automatically), extracts the card number, and triggers asynchronous background downloads using Playwright's `wait_for_event('download')` promise.
prevention_rule: When downloading Google Flow videos, always hide notifications via CSS style tag `section[aria-label='Notifications alt+T'] { display: none !important; }` and use the rotating spot downloader script which reads card numbers from the bottom up to capture them sequentially in 1080p.
error: Dynamic card shifting and notification toast intercepts caused Playwright downloads to fail or download incorrect files
prevention: Always hide the notifications container via CSS, use Playwright's `wait_for_event('download')` for background triggers, and scan card numbers dynamically starting from the bottom of the grid.

tried: Downloading and organizing Google Flow videos programmatically
wrong_because: Chrome DevTools MCP server occupies port 9222, causing external Playwright scripts to get 403 Forbidden. Also, programmatic right-click on draggable elements fails to open React dropdowns unless targeting the inner button element.
fix: Aligned with Wayne on a Split-Labor Handoff Protocol: the agent does script checks, generation, and Sequential UI Renaming (1 to 50, bottom to top), then STOPS. Wayne manually downloads and moves files to collection. The agent resumes later for B-rolls.
prevention: Always stop after video generation and renaming. Never run local Playwright downloader scripts unless requested. Hand off to Wayne for the download phase.
prevention_rule: Always stop after video generation and renaming. Never run local Playwright downloader scripts unless requested. Hand off to Wayne for the download phase.

tried: Importing corrected subtitles SRT file onto the Resolve timeline
wrong_because: SRT was generated with absolute timecodes starting at 01:00:00. Appending it with recordFrame set to the timeline start frame (86400 frames / 01:00:00) shifted subtitles by another 1 hour, placing them at 02:00:00.
error: Subtitles shifted by double offset on timeline to 02:00:00
fix: Regenerated the SRT file with relative timecodes starting at 00:00:00 (subtracting 172800 frames from the 2-hour shifted dump or 86400 frames from the original dump), deleted the subtitle track, added a new subtitle track, and appended the relative SRT at recordFrame=86400.
prevention: Always generate SRT files with 0-hour relative timecodes (starting at 00:00:00,000) when importing to DaVinci Resolve. Append them with recordFrame set to the timeline start frame (e.g. 86400) to align with timeline start (01:00:00:00).
prevention_rule: Always generate SRT files with 0-hour relative timecodes (starting at 00:00:00,000) when importing to DaVinci Resolve. Append them with recordFrame set to the timeline start frame (e.g. 86400) to align with timeline start (01:00:00:00).

tried: Providing YouTube video metadata with arrow formatting
wrong_because: Used '──>' as arrows for links in the YouTube description. YouTube strictly blocks angled brackets (< and >) in titles and descriptions to prevent HTML/script injection.
error: YouTube Studio rejects saving the description, giving an invalid character/angled bracket error.
fix: Replaced '──>' with a safe Unicode arrow character '➡' which doesn't trigger the HTML injection blocks.
prevention: Never use angled brackets (< or >) in YouTube video descriptions or titles. Use safe Unicode arrows like ➡ instead.
prevention_rule: NEVER use angled brackets (< or >) in YouTube video titles or descriptions. Use safe Unicode arrows like ➡ instead.

tried: Automating WordPress post creation and SEO metadata settings
wrong_because: Standard UI DOM click automation in Gutenberg is fragile due to frequent WordPress block editor updates, and setting Rank Math fields directly in React inputs via standard `.value` assignment fails because it doesn't dispatch synthetic React state events.
error: Fragile block editor clicks and Rank Math inputs failing to save values.
fix: Used direct Gutenberg and Rank Math JavaScript Redux APIs via `wp.data` and `wp.apiFetch` in the page console: `wp.data.dispatch('core/editor')` and `wp.data.dispatch('rank-math')` to programmatically and cleanly set blocks, SEO title, keywords, meta description, categories, tags, and upload featured media.
prevention: For WordPress Gutenberg and Rank Math automation, always use direct Redux state dispatches via wp.data and wp.apiFetch in the browser context rather than simulating raw mouse clicks and input entries.
prevention_rule: For WordPress Gutenberg and Rank Math automation, always use direct Redux state dispatches via wp.data and wp.apiFetch in the browser context rather than simulating raw mouse clicks and input entries.

tried: Deploying theme updates to GitHub repositories
wrong_because: GitHub repository paths were mapped backwards between Keystone Possibilities and Keystone Recomposition
fix: Map Keystone Recomposition to the second-website repository, and Keystone Possibilities to the construction-website repository.
prevention_rule: Map Keystone Recomposition to the second-website repository, and Keystone Possibilities to the construction-website repository.

tried: Updating WordPress themes via WP Pusher
wrong_because: Themes failed to update due to WP Pusher license expiration or directory locks, throwing critical errors
fix: Access WordPress Recovery Mode via the admin email link, update the WP Pusher plugin/license to restore connection, and verify the push.
prevention_rule: Access WordPress Recovery Mode via the admin email link, update the WP Pusher plugin/license to restore connection, and verify the push.

tried: Embedding video players with raw custom HTML blocks in WordPress
wrong_because: Raw HTML blocks are brittle, bypass theme styling controls, and require manual maintenance of iframe player layouts
fix: Use the native shortcode [keystone_video id='YOUTUBE_ID'] to dynamically render responsive, styled video players.
prevention_rule: Use the native shortcode [keystone_video id='YOUTUBE_ID'] to dynamically render responsive, styled video players.
